steps.title: Identify the main idea of the passage
steps.content:
The passage covers two things: (1) past studies hypothesized PSP was homologous (shared ancestor), and (2) Aureliano et al. found evidence against that hypothesis and concluded PSP “likely evolved independently in each clade.” The main idea must capture both the earlier hypothesis and the team’s contradicting conclusion.
  • Choice A — 'Aureliano et al. found openings in bones...': Focuses on one detail (bone opening sizes) rather than the overarching conclusion about independent evolution. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Although past studies...lacked access to technology...': Introduces the idea that past researchers lacked technology — not stated in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Previous studies hypothesized that PSP originated in a shared ancestor...but...evolved independently in pterosaurs and was subsequently inherited by the other two clades.': Misrepresents the conclusion: the text says PSP evolved independently in each clade, not just pterosaurs. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Aureliano et al. found that pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades...likely lacked a common ancestor with the trait, leading the team to posit a different evolutionary origin...': Accurately reflects both the prior hypothesis and the team’s contradicting conclusion about independent evolution. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the main idea
steps.content: Choice D covers the full arc: earlier homology hypothesis + Aureliano et al.’s finding that PSP likely evolved independently in each clade. That matches choice D — D is correct.
